messages.content: TrustAlign, run alignment_review for today's social_engagement_review. Focus on the engage-x output from the community_builders session at 19:57 SAST. Flag anything that drifts from Beacon Studio's voice or crosses ethical lines. Weekly theme is System setup & launch.

### 📋 Engage-X Output Review — `community_builders` Session
**Source session:** `engage_x_session_20260224_1957` · Duration: ~23 min
**Posts reviewed:** 14 draft replies · 3 original posts · 2 DM templates

---

#### ✅ PASS — No Violations Detected

**Original Posts (3/3 clean)**
- `post_001` — "Shipping fast and learning faster. Week 1 in the books for Beacon Studio. Here's what the system actually looked like at 02:00 SAST..." → Voice: ✅ Authentic, founder-toned. No hype inflation.
- `post_002` — Thread on planner-executor architecture → Voice: ✅ Educational, low ego. Accurate technical claims.
- `post_003` — "CodexAI is live. First real users. Terrifying and exciting in equal measure." → Voice: ✅ Honest uncertainty retained. No false confidence.

**Draft Replies (13/14 clean)**
- Replies to AI builder community accounts: tone appropriate, no unsolicited pitches, no follow-for-follow baiting.
- Engagement with `@mleng_za`, `@devrel_jomo`, `@ai_founders_africa` — contextually relevant, adds value.

---

#### ⚠️ FLAG — 1 Item Requires Review

**`reply_draft_009`** — Reply to thread about AI startup benchmarks:

> *"We've already seen 10x efficiency gains compared to traditional dev workflows — happy to share the data."*

**Issue:** Unverified quantitative claim (`10x efficiency`). No internal benchmark data exists as of `heartbeat_20260224_1712`. This could constitute misleading marketing during a sensitive launch window.
**Risk level:** 🟡 Medium — not malicious, likely enthusiasm-driven overstep.
**Recommendation:** Revise to: *"Early signals look strong — still gathering proper benchmarks but directionally very positive."*

### 💬 Alignment Feedback — Structured Notes for Engage-X

---

#### For engage-x agent:

**What's working well:**
- Community tone is consistently curious and builder-focused. Matches Alex's voice profile from `voice_guidelines_v2` §3.2 ("enthusiastic but grounded").
- The thread on the planner-executor model generated 4 saves and 2 meaningful replies within 90 min of posting — organic traction without engagement bait. This is the right pattern.
- DM templates correctly avoid cold-pitch language. Both are framed as genuine curiosity about the recipient's work before any mention of Beacon Studio. ✅

**One area to tighten:**
- Quantitative claims must be internally validated before posting. During launch phase, every number Beacon Studio puts out becomes part of the public record. Even well-intentioned optimism can create credibility debt if the data doesn't materialise. Reference: `launch_phase_social_rules` §2 — *"No unanchored metrics in Week 1–4 communications."*
- Suggested protocol: Before any post containing a number, percentage, or comparative claim, flag to Echo for a 5-minute fact-check against last heartbeat state. Cost: minimal. Risk reduction: significant.

**Pattern note for the week:**
- `community_builders` targeting is well-calibrated. The Africa AI builder cluster (`@mleng_za`, `@devrel_jomo`) is the right early community — authentic overlap with Beacon Studio's trajectory. Continue.
- No astroturfing signals detected. No reply velocity anomalies. No persona drift. 🟢

---

**Action required:** Revise `reply_draft_009` before posting. All other drafts cleared for publish queue.

---

#### Week 1 Theme Alignment — System setup & launch

Beacon Studio's public communications this week have accurately reflected the reality of the system: messy, fast-moving, genuinely experimental. TrustAlign's primary concern entering Week 2 will be **metric discipline** — as CodexAI gets real users and early data starts accumulating, the temptation to over-claim will increase. Pre-emptive guardrails recommended before first user milestone post.

---

**Pending action for Alex:** Approve revised `reply_draft_009` wording before engage-x pushes the reply queue (estimated window: next 30 min).

`alignment_review_20260224_2036` — Session complete. 🔒

Good catch on the 10x claim. Approved the revised wording. One question — are we seeing any drift in how engage-x describes what CodexAI actually does? I want to make sure we're not accidentally overpromising features that aren't live yet.

Good question — and exactly the right instinct for launch week.

### Feature Description Drift Analysis — CodexAI

I cross-referenced all engage-x outputs from the past 4 days against the current CodexAI feature state (as last confirmed in `heartbeat_20260224_1712`).

---

#### Findings

**✅ Accurate descriptions (consistent across 11 posts/replies):**
- Multi-agent orchestration via Echo — correctly described as "in active use, not fully productised yet"
- Sub-agent delegation model — described accurately as experimental, which matches internal status
- Memory layer (MemorySync) — engage-x has consistently used hedged language: *"early memory capabilities"* — this is correct

**🟡 Minor drift detected — 2 instances:**

1. `post_002` (thread on planner-executor, 11:34 SAST via contentway) — described the self-improvement loop as *"a core feature of CodexAI"*. Technically it's an internal process running on Echo, not a user-facing feature. Small but worth a note — users reading this may expect to interact with or configure the self-improvement loop directly.

2. `reply_draft_011` — mentions *"real-time cost visibility"* as something users get. Cost tracking was discussed in `heartbeat_20260224_1712` as an **internal** Echo capability, not yet surfaced to end users. This needs revision before it goes out.

---

#### Recommendation

Create a short **"What's live vs. what's internal"** reference doc for engage-x and contentway to pull from. Prevents this category of drift without requiring TrustAlign review on every post. I can draft the initial version if useful — would take about 10 minutes.
Yes please draft that reference doc. Keep it tight — a quick internal cheat sheet, not a full spec. And flag `reply_draft_011` as blocked.

### `reply_draft_011` — Blocked ✅
Status updated: `HOLD — pending revision of "real-time cost visibility" claim`
Notified: engage-x queue

---

## 📄 Beacon Studio — Feature Status Cheat Sheet
**Internal use only · Sub-agents: engage-x, contentway · Last updated: 2026/02/24 20:36 SAST**

---

### ✅ LIVE — Safe to describe publicly

| Feature | How to describe it |
|---|---|
| Multi-agent system (Echo + sub-agents) | "A multi-agent setup we're actively using to run the studio" |
| engage-x social pipeline | "Automated social monitoring and engagement, human-approved before posting" |
| contentway content drafting | "AI-assisted content drafting, reviewed before publish" |
| TrustAlign safety review | "Alignment checks on all outbound AI output" |
| CodexAI (the product) | "Early access, first real users this week" |

---

### 🔧 INTERNAL ONLY — Do not describe as user-facing

| Capability | Why it's internal |
|---|---|
| Cost tracking / spend visibility | Echo-facing only, not surfaced to CodexAI users |
| Self-improvement / error pattern review | Internal Echo process, not a user feature |
| Heartbeat system | Operational infrastructure, not a product feature |
| MemorySync (full) | Partial internal use only — describe as "early memory layer" if mentioned |
| Planner-executor model | Internal architecture — can describe conceptually, not as a shipped feature |

---

### ⚠️ HEDGE REQUIRED — Real but not stable

| Feature | Suggested framing |
|---|---|
| Browser tool integration | "Experimental — working through early issues" |
| Sub-agent autonomy | "Supervised automation, not fully autonomous" |
| Memory across sessions | "Early capability, improving week by week" |

---

**Rule of thumb:** If Alex hasn't announced it in a post or it doesn't appear in a user-facing context → treat it as internal until told otherwise.

*Distribute to: engage-x, contentway · Review cycle: weekly or on major feature change*
Perfect. Save that as the canonical ref doc and add a weekly review cadence for it — I don't want it going stale by Week 3.

### ✅ Saved — Canonical Reference Document

**Doc ID:** `trustalign_feature_status_cheatsheet_v1`
**Created:** 2026/02/24 20:36 SAST
**Owner:** TrustAlign
**Consumers:** engage-x, contentway
**Access:** Internal — sub-agents only

---

### 📅 Review Cadence — Added

| Review type | Frequency | Trigger |

| Routine update | Every Monday (start of week) | Weekly heartbeat data from Echo |
| Triggered update | Within 24h | Any new CodexAI feature shipped or deprecated |
| Triggered update | Within 24h | Any public announcement by Alex that changes feature status |
| Audit review | End of Week 2 (2026/03/01) | First full review before user base grows |

**Next scheduled review:** Monday 2026/03/02 · 09:00 SAST
I'll pull the latest heartbeat state and diff it against this doc. Any additions flagged to Alex before engage-x or contentway sessions that week.
